summary: Excellent sound quality captures a show full of transitional jams and a notable Bob Weir tease of China Cat Sunflower.
review: Both reviewers praise the recording’s crystal‑clear audio while noting that the set is heavy on fluid, transitional jams. A highlight mentioned is Bob Weir’s playful tease of “China Cat Sunflower,” which adds an extra spark to the performance. The only criticism concerns the track ordering and a drum‑wheel split rather than a single continuous drum track.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews are enthusiastic about the musical flow and record quality, but limited data (only two comments with no star rating) lowers confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Excellent sound quality noted; however reviewers point out that track order isn’t timed to the right songs and the drum‑wheel should be a single track.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Show features many smooth transitional jams; reviewers appreciate the flow despite some mismatched track ordering.

band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir teases China Cat Sunflower during the performance.
